acceptance_targets: [CH17_CONTINUITY_FIRE_CAUSE] Pass if a cold reader can read the revised material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:111-119 and state both the actual cause of the fire and Martin's false official explanation without contradiction, and the actual cause matches the boundary pack's cigarette-and-propane-line version. Fail if the heater remains both the real cause and the cover story.

[CH17_SANITIZED_CONFESSION_CUTAWAY] Pass if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:141-145 lets a cold reader state the trigger, the killing action, one bodily detail of Cliff's resistance or collapse, and why Tommy knows Cliff's death was homicide. Fail if the scene still jumps from an unfinished sentence to Paul's abstract reconstruction.

[CH17_PAYOFF_OVERSTATEMENT_SPRAWL] Pass if, after the revised house-break sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:171-205, the rest of the chapter advances through distinct beats only: ultimatum, Tommy's response or non-response, Paul's return to Joel, and the overnight suspension. Fail if any paragraph after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:205 merely restates Martin's protectiveness or Tommy's dependency without adding a new choice, consequence, or visible state change.

[PROSE_FORMULA_CH17] Pass if chapter 17 contains at most one 'the specific' and at most one 'Not X' fragment, with all other instances replaced by varied constructions. Fail if two or more of either template remain.

[MOTIF_PUNCT_CH17] Pass if chapter 17 contains at most one loon reference without descriptive elaboration of the call type, and the refrigerator reference at 4375 is either cut or reduced to background. Fail if two or more annotated loon calls remain.

[OVERSTATEMENT_CH17] Pass if the cited passages are compressed so that no two passages within chapter 17 restate the same thematic insight (Martin's protection was unsustainable / Paul will not repeat it) in substantially similar language, and the chapter's post-confrontation sections total no more than 60% of their original line count. Fail if the chapter still produces three or more separate restatements of the same insight.

[COMPOSED_DLG_CH17] Pass if Tommy's self-diagnosis at 4551 uses fragments and repetition per his style-bible stress profile rather than a compressed literary statement, Paul's line at 4599 arrives with friction rather than perfect compression, and the narrator at 4609 stays in Paul's sensory close-third rather than producing literary thesis. Fail if all three lines remain at their current level of polish.
[RED-025] Chapter 17 does not restate the dock's thirty-foot length or the eighty-yard path distance.
[CON-006] Chapter 17's reference to Joel's tenure matches the canonical number.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if a cold reader can read the revised material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:111-119 and state both the actual cause of the fire and Martin's false official explanation without contradiction, and the actual cause matches the boundary pack's cigarette-and-propane-line version. Fail if the heater remains both the real cause and the cover story.
findings.problem: The chapter introduces a hard continuity contradiction about the 2002 fire. Here Tommy says he knocked over a space heater and Martin later blamed a faulty heater and old wiring, but the allowed boundary context for Chapter 18 identifies the actual ignition as Tommy being drunk and careless with a cigarette near a propane line. Because both versions are presented as the substance of Martin's document and Tommy's memory, the book now has two incompatible causal accounts of the same canonical event.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:111-119 so Tommy's account cleanly separates the real ignition event from Martin's later cover story. If the canonical version is the cigarette and propane line described in the boundary pack, this scene cannot also make the knocked-over heater the real cause. Keep Martin's false electrical explanation only as the lie they told investigators, not as the underlying event.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passage at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:141-145 lets a cold reader state the trigger, the killing action, one bodily detail of Cliff's resistance or collapse, and why Tommy knows Cliff's death was homicide. Fail if the scene still jumps from an unfinished sentence to Paul's abstract reconstruction.
findings.problem: The chapter flinches exactly where it most needs scene presence. Tommy's account of killing Cliff stops at "and I —," and the narration immediately replaces the act with Paul's inferential summary about the dock, the depth, and the shoes. That is sanitization drift: the chapter asks the reader to weigh a murder confession, but it retreats from the physical sequence, the bodily resistance, and the instant Tommy knows he has crossed into homicide.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:141-145 so Tommy names the dock sequence in his own broken language rather than cutting away before the act. The revision should specify what Cliff said or did in the last exchange, what Tommy physically did first, how the water struggle or collapse actually went, and what concrete detail tells Paul this was murder rather than an accident. Keep the confession brief and ragged, but do not substitute atmospheric inference for the act itself.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, after the revised house-break sequence at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:171-205, the rest of the chapter advances through distinct beats only: ultimatum, Tommy's response or non-response, Paul's return to Joel, and the overnight suspension. Fail if any paragraph after snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:205 merely restates Martin's protectiveness or Tommy's dependency without adding a new choice, consequence, or visible state change.
findings.problem: The chapter reaches its emotional proof when Tommy processes the house as twenty-two years of watchfulness and dependence, but it keeps re-translating the same meaning afterward. Once the house reveal and Tommy's break have landed, later narration keeps paraphrasing the same thesis about Martin keeping Tommy, the house being sentence as well as gift, and Paul refusing Martin's role. That repetition weakens force instead of compounding it, which keeps the chapter below shortlist level.
findings.rewrite_direction: After snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:195-205, compress or replace the later explanatory material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:213-213, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:245-257, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:267-267, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_17.md:281-285. Preserve the must-land beats: Tommy breaks at the inheritance, Paul gives the 6 AM ultimatum, Tommy registers it, and Paul returns to Joel. Every paragraph after the break should either add a new decision, a new consequence, or a new observable change in pressure.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 17 contains at most one 'the specific' and at most one 'Not X' fragment, with all other instances replaced by varied constructions. Fail if two or more of either template remain.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4395 'Not hostile. Not the coaching volume, not the bluster. Just flat.'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4459 'Not a question. The way he'd write it on a form.'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4495 'the specific kind that comes from holding words back'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4503 'the specific weight of a bond'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4511 'the specific September smell'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4591 'the specific weather that meant September'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4593 'Not a question.'

evidence.excerpt: 4395: "Yeah." Tommy's voice was flat. Not hostile. Not the coaching volume, not the bluster. Just flat. The voice of a man who'd been sitting in a cold chair waiting for the thing he'd been waiting for. "I figured."
4459: "Ray Toomey died," Paul said. Not a question. The way he'd write it on a form. *Insured's account of the loss.*

4495: "Protected." Paul's throat ached, and the ache was the specific kind that comes from holding words back for too long and then releasing them into air that isn't warm enough to receive them. "He protected you, Tommy. His whole life. And he never told me."

4503: The sentence was out before Paul could qualify it. He would normally have softened it — *to be fair, it's not that simple, I don't mean it like that.* But the qualifiers were burned off and the sentence stood in the cold air between them and it was true. Martin loved Tommy more. Not more cleanly, not more simply, but more urgently — with the specific weight of a bond made in a burning cabin with a dead man in the next room. Paul's friendship with Martin was clean. Martin's bond with Tommy was something else entirely — love and guilt and obligation, twenty-two years of showing up for the person you saved or the person who owed you the saving, and neither of them ever being sure which was which.

4511: The wind was picking up off the lake. Paul could feel it on his face, cold and smelling of the water — organic, mineral, the specific September smell of a lake turning over, life decaying in the shallows as the temperature dropped. His socks were soaked through from the dew on the boards. He should have gone inside for shoes. He should have done a lot of things.

4591: Tommy looked at him for a long time. The wind off the lake pressed at their faces and Paul could smell the water and the pines and the cold coming in from the north, the specific weather that meant September in Minnesota was about to become October.
4593: "You're not going to do what he did," Tommy said. Not a question.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 4395: "Yeah." Tommy's voice was flat. Not hostile. Not the coaching volume, not the bluster. Just flat. The voice of a man who'd been sitting in a cold chair waiting for the thing he'd been waiting for. "I figured."

findings.problem: Chapter 17 is the worst offender in the manuscript — six instances of 'the specific' and three instances of 'Not X' in a single chapter. The density is acute: lines 4491-4511 contain two 'the specific' constructions within 20 lines, and the chapter uses 'Not a question' twice (4459 and 4593). This creates a mechanical prose texture in what should be the novel's most emotionally raw chapter.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4395, 4459, 4495, 4503, 4511, 4591, 4593. Keep at most one 'the specific' (4495 is the strongest — the held-words construction earns it). Eliminate all others. Keep at most one 'Not a question' in the chapter (4459 is more effective because it's Paul's voice). Replace 4593 with a direct observation of Tommy's declarative tone. At 4511, 'the specific September smell' should become concrete — name the decay, the cold, the turned leaves — without the formula.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if chapter 17 contains at most one loon reference without descriptive elaboration of the call type, and the refrigerator reference at 4375 is either cut or reduced to background. Fail if two or more annotated loon calls remain.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4375 'the refrigerator was in the loud phase of its cycle'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4567 'The loons had started again. The tremolo — the alarmed call'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4627 'the loons calling across the water in the dark. The wail this time'

evidence.excerpt: 4375: Paul left Joel with his legal pad and his cold coffee and went to the kitchen, where the refrigerator was in the loud phase of its cycle and the remains of somebody's sandwich sat on a plate nobody had washed. The kitchen smelled like old coffee and the faint cedar of Martin's cologne, which had somehow gotten into every room and refused to leave, the way Martin himself had gotten into every room and refused to leave, even now, even dead.

4567: The loons had started again. The tremolo — the alarmed call, the one that sounded like laughing if you didn't know what it was and sounded like warning if you did. Paul had heard it every September for twenty-five years. He'd hear it differently now.

4627: But Paul didn't look. He sat in Martin's chair with his eyes closed and his wet socks leaving marks on the hardwood floor and he listened to the loons calling across the water in the dark. The wail this time — the long, rising one, the sound that carried over the lake like grief looking for a place to land. Joel turned a page on his legal pad. The refrigerator clicked off. The house went quiet, the way the house had been quiet at one-thirty on Saturday morning when Martin's heart stopped, the way it would be quiet tomorrow at six when Tommy walked down the stairs and sat across from Detective Mallick in the kitchen and said whatever he was going to say.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 4567: The loons had started again. The tremolo — the alarmed call, the one that sounded like laughing if you didn't know what it was and sounded like warning if you did. Paul had heard it every September for twenty-five years. He'd hear it differently now.

findings.problem: Chapter 17's Paul-Tommy confrontation is the emotional climax of the novel. The loon calls at 4567 and 4627 both describe the call's quality in detail ('the tremolo — the alarmed call' and 'The wail this time — the long, rising one'). This level of loon-call analysis in the novel's most dramatic chapter converts ambient detail into narrated ornament.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4567, 4627. Keep at most one loon reference in the chapter and strip the descriptive elaboration — let the sound be itself, not an annotated field guide entry. At 4567, the parenthetical about what the call means ('sounded like laughing if you didn't know... sounded like warning if you did') is the narrator explaining the motif's symbolism. Cut the explanation. At 4627, 'the wail this time' implies the narrator is categorizing loon calls for the reader. Let the sound arrive without taxonomy.

findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4377 — 13-line paragraph of Paul standing at the sliding glass door, restating his grief and his ignorance in summary form; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4503-4507 — two paragraphs analyzing the Martin-Tommy bond's nature after Tommy has already said 'He loved you too'; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4547 — 6-line paragraph describing Tommy as 'not a loud man or a dangerous man or a coach or a killer or any of the things the surface had said he was,' restating the chapter's central recognition; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4587-4599 — Paul's massive interior paragraph (12+ lines) analyzing Martin's protection system, repeating the word 'management' and restating the chapter's thesis about Paul-vs-Martin approaches to loyalty

evidence.excerpt: 4377: He stood at the sliding glass door. Through the glass, Tommy was perfectly still. The kitchen light threw a yellow rectangle on the deck boards behind him, and Tommy's shadow stretched long toward the railing, and Paul stood there looking at a man he'd known for twenty-five years and had apparently never known at all and tried to understand why his chest hurt the way it did. Not the Martin grief — that was older now, three days old, which in grief terms meant it had moved from the skin to the bone. This was a different hurt. This was the hurt of having to do the thing he was about to do, and knowing Martin would have done it differently, and knowing Martin's way was wrong.

4503: The sentence was out before Paul could qualify it. He would normally have softened it — *to be fair, it's not that simple, I don't mean it like that.* But the qualifiers were burned off and the sentence stood in the cold air between them and it was true. Martin loved Tommy more. Not more cleanly, not more simply, but more urgently — with the specific weight of a bond made in a burning cabin with a dead man in the next room. Paul's friendship with Martin was clean. Martin's bond with Tommy was something else entirely — love and guilt and obligation, twenty-two years of showing up for the person you saved or the person who owed you the saving, and neither of them ever being sure which was which.

4505: "Yeah," Tommy said. Not arguing. "He did."
4506: 
4507: Something moved across Tommy's face then — not an expression Paul could name, exactly, but a shift, a loosening, as if a muscle that had been contracted since 2002 had released. It looked like exhaustion. It looked like grief. And underneath both of those, barely visible, it looked like relief. The relief of being seen. Twenty-two years of performing bluntness and simplicity, of making himself small because smallness w...

4547: His sentence collapsed. He was sitting in Martin's chair on Martin's deck looking at Martin's lake and his hands were on his knees and his shoulders were folded inward and he was not a loud man or a dangerous man or a coach or a killer or any of the things the surface had said he was. He was a man who had started a fire when he was twenty-five and had been kept whole ever since by another man's attention, and the attention was over.

4587: Paul looked at him. Tommy in the Adirondack chair. Martin's chair, Martin's deck, Martin's paint peeling on the armrests that Paul had rested his arms on a hundred times while they argued about the roster and drank too much and talked the way men talk when the drinks have been flowing for hours and the lake is flat and the night is doing the thing nights do up here, where the darkness is bigger than the darkness in the city and the silence is a physical presence and the years of friendship accumulate like sediment until you can't see the bottom anymore. All those nights. All those conversations on this deck about nothing and everything and the Vikings' chances and whose marriage was holding and who looked rough this year. And underneath all of it, underneath every one of those nights, Tommy carrying the fire and Martin carrying Tommy and Paul carrying nothing, knowing nothing, sitting in the chair next to the biggest secret in the group and thinking the biggest problem was Martin's draft strategy.
4588: 
4589: "Then I tell her," Paul said. "In the morning. Six AM."

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 4377: He stood at the sliding glass door. Through the glass, Tommy was perfectly still. The kitchen light threw a yellow rectangle on the deck boards behind him, and Tommy's shadow stretched long toward the railing, and Paul stood there looking at a man he'd known for twenty-five years and had apparently never known at all and tried to understand why his chest hurt the way it did. Not the Martin grief — that was older now, three days old, which in grief terms meant it had moved from the skin to the bone. This was a different hurt. This was the hurt of having to do the thing he was about to do, and knowing Martin would have done it differently, and knowing Martin's way was wrong.

findings.problem: Chapter 17 is the novel's emotional climax but spends approximately 40% of its post-confrontation pages restating insights the scene has already earned. The Paul-Tommy dialogue is strong; the narrated interiority following each exchange explains what the dialogue already showed. The chapter produces at least four separate extended restatements of the same core insight: Martin's protection of Tommy was real but unsustainable, and Paul will not repeat it.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4377, 4503-4507, 4547, 4587-4599. At 4377, cut to 5-6 lines — keep the physical detail (Paul at the door, Tommy in the chair) and cut the grief-summary. At 4503-4507, cut the analytical paragraph that follows 'He loved you more' — the sentence stands alone. At 4547, cut entirely — the prior scene has earned this recognition without narrator summary. At 4587-4599, compress by 60% — keep Paul's decision (he will not be Martin) and cut the restatement of why Martin's system failed, which the reader has now heard three times.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if Tommy's self-diagnosis at 4551 uses fragments and repetition per his style-bible stress profile rather than a compressed literary statement, Paul's line at 4599 arrives with friction rather than perfect compression, and the narrator at 4609 stays in Paul's sensory close-third rather than producing literary thesis. Fail if all three lines remain at their current level of polish.
findings.evidence: snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4551 'Without that, Deck, I'm just the guy who killed somebody. That's all I am' — Tommy produces a perfectly compressed self-diagnosis that contradicts his style bible's 'short declarative sentences' and his avoidance of abstract language; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4599 'Martin would've given you the rest of your life' — Paul produces a line so compressed and perfectly timed it reads as composed rather than spoken; snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4609 'The gift that was also a sentence. The house that was also a monument to a bond that was never friendship, not really' — narrator analysis of the preceding scene that reads as authored thesis rather than close-third Paul

evidence.excerpt: 4551: "That's the thing," Tommy said. Quieter still, but steadier. The trembling in his hands had stopped. His body had settled into the chair the way a structure settles after the shaking stops — not repaired, not whole, but done moving. Bearing what it was going to bear. "Martin kept me. That's what it was. Twenty-two years on the roster. Making sure I showed up, making sure I didn't disappear, making sure I sat on this deck and acted like a guy who was still part of something. And I hated it and I needed it and without it — " He looked at Paul. His eyes were clear. "Without that, Deck, I'm just the guy who killed somebody. That's all I am."

4599: "Martin would've given you the rest of your life." The sentence left Paul's mouth with a weight he hadn't expected, and he felt it land — not on Tommy but on himself, in the place where his grief for Martin had been quietly transforming for three days into something harder and more clear. He was not going to be Martin. He was not going to spend the next twenty-two years managing Tommy's guilt and the body at the dock and the name of a man who died in a fire in Washburn County while the group gathered every September on this deck and pretended the foundation underneath them was solid. Martin did that because Martin loved Tommy, and because Martin was the kind of man who, once he decided to carry something, carried it until it killed him. His heart gave out on Saturday at one-thirty in the morning and Paul would never know whether the twenty-two years of carrying Tommy's secret had anything to do with the specific way Martin's heart was built, but he knew it had something to do with the kind of life Martin lived — the vigilance, the management, the generosity that was always, always covering something. Paul loved Martin. But he was not Martin. He was Paul Decker, claims adjuste...

4609: Tommy didn't answer. Paul looked at him one more time. Tommy in the Adirondack chair, the lake behind him, the last of the kitchen light on half his face. The other half in shadow. He looked like what he was — a forty-seven-year-old man sitting on the deck of a house he'd just learned he'd inherited from the friend who'd loved him the way you love the person whose worst moment you witnessed and whose life you rearranged your own to protect. The gift that was also a sentence. The house that was also a monument to a bond that was never friendship, not really, or was friendship but the kind that grows in the soil of something terrible and takes the shape of the terrible thing even as it reaches for the light.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 4551: "That's the thing," Tommy said. Quieter still, but steadier. The trembling in his hands had stopped. His body had settled into the chair the way a structure settles after the shaking stops — not repaired, not whole, but done moving. Bearing what it was going to bear. "Martin kept me. That's what it was. Twenty-two years on the roster. Making sure I showed up, making sure I didn't disappear, making sure I sat on this deck and acted like a guy who was still part of something. And I hated it and I needed it and without it — " He looked at Paul. His eyes were clear. "Without that, Deck, I'm just the guy who killed somebody. That's all I am."

findings.problem: Tommy's self-diagnosis at 4551 is too articulate for his style bible profile — under extreme stress, his 'sentences collapse into fragments and repetitions,' not into compressed literary self-assessments. Paul's line at 4599 is perfectly timed in a way that real speech under duress is not. The narrator commentary at 4609 admires its own formulation rather than staying in Paul's head.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:4551 — Let Tommy's self-assessment arrive in his own register: fragmented, repeated, physical. Something like: 'Without Martin, I'm — look, I'm the guy. That's what I am. The guy who — ' and let it trail off. Revise 4599 — Add friction: 'Martin would've — he would've let you live with this forever. He would've just kept — ' let Paul lose the sentence. Revise 4609 — Cut the narrator's 'gift that was also a sentence' formulation. Paul's close-third shouldn't produce this level of literary polishing; stay in sensation and observation.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: The comedy is the creative risk and the creative achievement. These are men in their late forties who talk the way men in their late forties talk when there are no women present and the drinks have been flowing for hours: crude, raunchy, profane, sexually explicit, casually cruel, and occasionally accidentally beautiful. Do not clean this up. Do not soften the language. Do not make them witty where they would be vulgar. Do not make them eloquent where they would be clumsy. The profanity is not decoration — it is the texture of the friendship. When the second death breaks the comic register, the absence of that profane warmth should feel like a loss, not a relief. The murder, when it arrives, should be rendered with physical specificity — the water, the struggle, the sounds. The grief should be specific. The shame should be specific. The twenty-two-year-old guilt should be embodied, not summarized. If a scene makes the writer uncomfortable, that is a signal to stay in it longer, not to cut away.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Every euphemism for death, sex, bodily function, emotional state, or financial desperation must be replaced with the direct term or a character-specific avoidance that itself reveals something about the character. Cliff saying 'restructure' when he means 'I'm broke' is character work. The narrator describing Cliff as 'facing financial challenges' is sanitization.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the absolute norm. These are informal men in their late forties who have been friends for twenty-five years. Uncontracted forms should appear only for deliberate emphasis, Joel's occasional legal precision, or Mallick's public-persona overcorrection. If two consecutive lines of dialogue contain uncontracted forms and neither character has a stated reason for formality, at least one is wrong.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: No two consecutive chapters should have the same structural rhythm. After a dialogue-heavy chapter, lead the next with interiority or description. After an interview chapter, give the reader a memory or a quiet observation. After a comic set piece, let the next chapter breathe. The scene counts vary (2-3) and should feel varied — a two-scene chapter after a three-scene chapter creates natural pacing shift. The tonal break at Chapter 14 should feel like a gear change, not a gradual dimming.
prose_style_profile.diction: Muscular, specific, unpretentious. No literary posturing. The prose should sound like intelligent conversation, not workshopped fiction. Prefer concrete nouns over abstract ones. Prefer active constructions. Avoid decorative metaphor — when a metaphor appears, it should be load-bearing. The comedy demands precision: funny is specific, and vague is never funny.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Prestige-literary inflation — do not elevate the comic register into literary solemnity. A man flushing Viagra while a detective knocks on the door is funny. The prose should be funny. Do not describe it as if it were a scene from Dostoevsky.

Comedic exhaustion — do not let every paragraph contain a joke. Comedy needs rhythm, which means it needs non-comic beats to land against. Let some paragraphs be straightforwardly descriptive or emotional.
Interior monologue sprawl — close third is not stream of consciousness. The POV character's interiority should be selective and purposeful, not an open channel of every thought.
Simultaneous narration — do not describe characters 'simultaneously' feeling multiple things. Choose the dominant feeling and let the undertone be implicit.
Forensic procedure porn — this is not a police procedural. Keep investigation details specific but brief. The comedy and character are the vehicle, not the forensics.

Metaphor creep — do not let the same metaphor template appear more than twice in a chapter. If you've used a sports metaphor, a water metaphor, and a fire metaphor, check that none of them are doing the same structural work.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV. Each chapter is locked to one character's interiority. No omniscient intrusions. The prose should take on the coloring of the POV character without becoming parody — Nate's chapters move faster and list more, Doug's chapters are quieter and more observational, Mallick's chapters shift between her two registers.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Conversational propulsion, not literary drift. The prose should move like someone telling you this story — not rushed, but never self-admiring. Vary sentence length aggressively: long compound sentences for the comic set pieces, short declarative hits for the turns. The humor lives in rhythm as much as content — the timing of a punchline is a prose-level decision.
sensory_bias: auditory — conversations, lake sounds, house creaks, the specific silence of a house where someone died
proprioceptive — hangovers, physical discomfort, adrenaline, the specific sensation of a body that hasn't slept
olfactory — booze, lake water, morning coffee, the basement must, the casserole
